# HTX.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the HTX REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports Spot, Spot Margin, Sub-Accounts, and USDT Futures. Part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem providing a unified abstraction across cryptocurrency exchanges.

HTX.Net, previously known as Huobi.Net, provides typed REST clients, WebSocket subscriptions, automatic reconnection, client-side rate limiting, local order book support, native AOT support, and the standard CryptoExchange.Net result model. REST methods return `HttpResult<T>` / `HttpResult`, WebSocket subscriptions return `WebSocketResult<UpdateSubscription>`, Spot WebSocket request/query methods return `QueryResult<T>` / `QueryResult`, and selected shared helper methods return `ExchangeCallResult<T>`. Current version: 8.x. Targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/blob/master/README.md): Overview, installation, supported features, basic usage examples, full release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/HTX.Net/): Full documentation hub including client reference, WebSocket subscriptions, dependency injection, and shared interfaces
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/blob/master/docs/ai-api-map.md): Quick intent-to-method map for routing common user requests to the correct client member
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/blob/master/llms-full.txt): Detailed AI/LLM guide with code patterns, endpoint map, common mistakes, and anti-hallucination rules

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/tree/master/Examples/ai-friendly): Compact, fully runnable examples optimized for AI assistants and quick onboarding
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Client setup, ticker, account id and balances, limit order placement, order status check
- [USDT Futures Trading](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs): USDT futures cross margin with leverage, market order, position retrieval, position close
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Ticker subscription, kline subscription, account/order streams with proper teardown
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): Using CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis and shared capability discovery for exchange-agnostic code
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): HttpResult, WebSocketResult, QueryResult, and ExchangeCallResult patterns, retry logic, common HTX routing mistakes and their handling
- [Full Examples Repository](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/tree/master/Examples): Additional examples and integration patterns

## Reference

- [CryptoExchange.Net base library](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net): Base abstractions used by HTX.Net and other exchange libraries
- [CryptoClients.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoClients.Net): Bundle package providing unified access to all exchange libraries
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/JKorf.HTX.Net): Latest stable release on NuGet

## Optional

- [Discord Community](https://discord.gg/MSpeEtSY8t): Maintainer-supported Discord for questions and discussion
- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/JKorf/HTX.Net/issues): Bug reports and feature requests
- [GitHub Sponsors](https://github.com/sponsors/JKorf): Support the maintainer
